title: crinum ornatum var. zeylanicum
intro: Crinum ornatum var. zeylanicum is a plant species, commonly known as the 'Red Lily' or 'Wild Onion', belonging to the Amaryllidaceae family. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant effects. The health benefits of Crinum ornatum var. zeylanicum include treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its use in wound healing and as an antiseptic for skin infections. Traditional herbal preparations include infusions and decoctions, which can be made by boiling the plant's bulbs or leaves in water to create remedies for various ailments.
intro_study: According to "Immunopharmacology", crinum ornatum var. zeylanicum (considered a variant of Crinum asiaticum) may have anti-inflammatory benefits, as it was found to significantly reduce paw oedema in mice and exhibited anti-inflammatory activity through a mechanism that may involve anti-histaminic properties.
